description_long: This is a timber-framed barn with a prominent gable roof, featuring exposed wooden beams and a weathered, shingled upper section. The structure has a rectangular footprint and displays a traditional half-timbered pattern on its exterior walls, with infill panels between the wooden framework. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with what appears to be clay or terracotta tiles, some of which show signs of aging. The front facade includes a central entryway with a small overhang and a loft opening above, while the sides are largely solid with minimal fenestration. Windows are sparse and irregularly placed, with one or two small openings on the gable ends. The overall appearance suggests a historic European rural barn, likely used for storage or agricultural purposes.
description: A timber-framed barn with a steep gable roof, exposed beams, and a traditional half-timbered exterior. The structure features a central entry, loft opening, and minimal, irregular windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, loft opening above, and symmetrical timber framing.
description_view_1: Gable end view highlighting the steep roof pitch, half-timbered pattern, and a small window near the top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the long, mostly solid wall with a single small window and the full expanse of the tiled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end with similar timber framing and a small window, mirroring the other side.
attribute_door_style: single hinged barn door with a loft opening above
attribute_window_arrangement: irregularly placed small windows on gable ends and rear wall
